steps.title: Find both claims the passage makes
steps.content: The passage makes two distinct claims about community science: (1) it promotes community engagement by offering insight into a scientist's daily life, and (2) it greatly increases the amount of data that can be collected. Any answer that captures both claims accurately is the main idea.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — Sarah Whitcomb and colleagues used a community science approach in their butterfly research and were surprised at the effect this had on public understanding of the nature of scientists' work.: Whitcomb is only an example; the passage never says anyone was surprised. ✗
  • Choice B — A community science approach can increase public understanding of the nature of scientists' work and generate large amounts of data, benefiting both communities and researchers.: Covers both the engagement benefit and the data benefit the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice C — A community science approach can benefit communities by increasing public understanding of the nature of scientists' work, but it's less likely to help researchers obtain accurate data.: Contradicts the passage, which says the approach is "very effective" and greatly increases data. ✗
  • Choice D — A community science approach allows researchers to collect significantly more data even though it's unlikely to have much of an effect on public understanding of the nature of scientists' work.: The passage explicitly says it promotes community engagement, so dismissing that benefit is wrong. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
